mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0 percent, labor market slack signals weakening aggregate demand and corporate earnings deterioration, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns remain positive in over 65 percent of periods where UNRATE exceeds 5.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in real personal income and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer spending and construction activity, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 20-day returns with a Rank IC above 0.04 in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: In a high inflation environment, persistent CPIAUCSL readings above 270 force central banks to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity multiples and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are not significantly negative when CPIAUCSL exceeds 270, or the signal loses predictive power during disinflationary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production directly increases corporate output and revenue expectations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth shows zero or negative correlation with 20-day forward returns across multiple market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: When quarterly real GDP growth falls below 1.5 percent, aggregate demand contraction triggers earnings downgrades and risk-off positioning, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns remain positive in over 70 percent of quarters where GDPC1 growth is below 1.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with falling unemployment rates indicate a tightening labor market that supports wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal of PAYEMS increases and UNRATE decreases fails to predict positive 20-day returns with statistical significance in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: During stagflationary periods, declining industrial production alongside elevated inflation erodes profit margins and triggers defensive portfolio rebalancing, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no negative bias when INDPRO falls below 98 during high inflation periods, or the mechanism fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark.
